oneLiner.value: Managed headless browser cloud that lets AI agents and developers automate web interactions at scale without managing Chromium infrastructure.

value.shortDescription: Managed headless browser cloud that lets AI agents and developers automate web interactions at scale without managing Chromium infrastructure.
value.concept: Every AI startup building web-facing agents solves the same browser infrastructure problems independently — CAPTCHA handling, anti-bot fingerprinting, proxy rotation, session isolation, autoscaling — so Browserbase builds it once and sells it as a consumption-priced service, the same model Twilio applied to telephony and Stripe to payments.
value.serves: Primarily developers and engineering teams at AI-native startups and enterprises building agents that need to interact with the live web; secondarily non-technical business users via the no-code Director product.
value.mechanism: Customers connect via standard Playwright, Puppeteer, or Selenium code to Browserbase's serverless cloud, which provisions isolated Chromium sessions on demand with built-in CAPTCHA solving, stealth fingerprinting, proxy rotation, SOC-2/HIPAA compliance, and global data-center distribution; Stagehand (open-source SDK) adds natural-language act/extract/observe commands above Playwright; Director generates automation scripts from natural-language prompts for non-developers.

citations.snippet: Browserbase raised $40M Series B led by Notable Capital, with CRV and Kleiner Perkins. Serves over 1,000 companies and 20,000+ developers. Launched Director, a no-code automation tool.

citations.title: Browserbase reels in $40M for its browser automation tools - SiliconANGLE
citations.snippet: Notable Capital led Series B. Valued at $300M, 4x its September valuation. Platform spins up thousands of browsers in a fraction of a second, compatible with Puppeteer and Selenium. Stagehand lets developers combine traditional scripts with AI agents.

citations.title: Browserbase Raises $40M Series B, Launches AI Automation Tool
citations.snippet: 1,000+ customers including Commure, 11x, Perplexity, Vercel. 20,000+ developers, 50M browser sessions in 2025. $3M+ in revenue as of Series B, mostly from team expansion. Consumption-based pricing. First salesperson hired day of Series B announcement. Angel investors include Guillermo Rauch (Vercel CEO), Jeff Lawson (Twilio), Patrick Collison (Stripe), Aaron Levie (Box).

citations.title: Browserbase: Web Browser Automation Company Raises $21 Million (Series A)
citations.snippet: Series A of $21M co-led by CRV and Kleiner Perkins, with Okta Ventures and Reed McGinley-Stempel. Raised in less than nine months after $6.5M seed. Launched Stagehand open-source AI-powered web automation SDK at Series A.

citations.title: Browserbase Raises $27M to Fix the Infrastructure Problem Killing Web-Browsing Agents at Scale | AgentMarketCap
citations.snippet: Independent technical analysis. Founded by Paul Klein IV in SF. Stagehand exposes act/extract/observe natural language commands above Playwright. Competitors include Steel.dev, Playwright-MCP, Browserless. Adobe Analytics: AI agent traffic to US retail surged 4,700% YoY (July 2025).

citations.title: Building the Browser Infrastructure for AI Agents with the Founder of Browserbase | Notable Capital
citations.snippet: Notable Capital led Series B. Glenn Solomon, managing director: 'If you build a great service that provides for and anticipates the needs that developers have, it can be a very compelling value proposition.' Klein analogizes to Vercel/Stripe/Twilio infrastructure model.

citations.title: Browserbase Raises $6.5M in Seed Funding
citations.snippet: $6.5M seed led by Kleiner Perkins, with Basecase Capital and AI Grant. Announced 2024-06-07.

citations.snippet: Law firm confirmation of Notable Capital as lead investor in Browserbase Series B.

citations.title: Browserbase Secures $40M Series B Round, Launches No-Code Tool | Built In
citations.snippet:
{"id":"https://builtin.com/articles/browserbase-announces-40m-series-b-funding-20250618","title":"Browserbase Secures $40M Series B Round, Launches No-Code Tool | Built In","url":"https://builtin.com/articles/browserbase-announces-40m-series-b-funding-20250618","publishedDate":"2025-06-18T16:29:32.000Z","author":"Mia Goulart","text":"Browserbase Secures $40M Series B Round, Launches No-Code Tool | Built In

Photo: Browserbase

Browserbase, a California-based browser infrastructure platform for agentic software, has raised $40 million in a Series B funding round led by Notable Capital, with continued support from existing investors CRV and Kleiner Perkins.

According to a news release, the funding coincides with the launch of Director, a new no-code tool that enables non-technical users to build and run automations simply by describing what they want to accomplish.

# Browserbase: Web Browser Automation Company Raises $21 Million (Series A)

By Amit Chowdhry • Nov 12, 2024

Browserbase: Web Browser Automation Company Raises $21 Million (Series A)

Browserbase, a leader in web browser automation, announced it raised $21 million in Series A funding co-led by CRV and Kleiner Perkins, with participation from Okta Ventures and Reed McGinley-Stempel, CEO of Stych.

citations.snippet:
{"id":"https://builtin.com/articles/browserbase-announces-40m-series-b-funding-20250618","title":"Browserbase Secures $40M Series B Round, Launches No-Code Tool | Built In","url":"https://builtin.com/articles/browserbase-announces-40m-series-b-funding-20250618","publishedDate":"2025-06-18T16:29:32.000Z","author":"Mia Goulart","text":"Browserbase Secures $40M Series B Round, Launches No-Code Tool | Built In

Photo: Browserbase

Browserbase, a California-based browser infrastructure platform for agentic software, has raised $40 million in a Series B funding round led by Notable Capital, with continued support from existing investors CRV and Kleiner Perkins.

According to a news release, the funding coincides with the launch of Director, a new no-code tool that enables non-technical users to build and run automations simply by describing what they want to accomplish.

### Browserbase reels in $40M for its browser automation tools

SHARE

Browserbase Inc., a startup focused on automating tasks that involve interacting with webpages, has closed a $40 million funding round.

Notable Capital led the Series B investment.

# Browserbase: Web Browser Automation Company Raises $21 Million (Series A)

By Amit Chowdhry • Nov 12, 2024

Browserbase: Web Browser Automation Company Raises $21 Million (Series A)

Browserbase, a leader in web browser automation, announced it raised $21 million in Series A funding co-led by CRV and Kleiner Perkins, with participation from Okta Ventures and Reed McGinley-Stempel, CEO of Stych.
